audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra sexual-boundary text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Egypt/Canaan contrast, statutes/judgments, kinship exposure prohibitions, Molech, male-male text, animal boundary, land defilement rhetoric. Strong anti-weaponization and anti-queer-hatred safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: I am YHWH your Divine.
Like doings of land of Egypt where you dwelt you shall not do, and like doings of land of Canaan where I am bringing you you shall not do; in their statutes you shall not walk.
My judgments you shall do, and my statutes you shall keep, to walk in them. I am YHWH your Divine.
You shall keep my statutes and my judgments, which a human shall do and live by them. I am YHWH.
No man shall draw near to any flesh of his flesh/kin to uncover nakedness. I am YHWH.
Nakedness of your father and nakedness of your mother you shall not uncover; she is your mother; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
Nakedness of your father's wife you shall not uncover; it is nakedness of your father.
Nakedness of your sister, daughter of your father or daughter of your mother, born at home or born outside, their nakedness you shall not uncover.
Nakedness of your son's daughter or your daughter's daughter, their nakedness you shall not uncover; for theirs is your nakedness.
Nakedness of daughter of your father's wife, born to your father, she is your sister; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
Nakedness of your father's sister you shall not uncover; she is flesh of your father.
Nakedness of your mother's sister you shall not uncover; she is flesh of your mother.
Nakedness of your father's brother you shall not uncover; to his wife you shall not draw near; she is your aunt.
Nakedness of your daughter-in-law you shall not uncover; she is wife of your son; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
Nakedness of your brother's wife you shall not uncover; it is nakedness of your brother.
Nakedness of a woman and her daughter you shall not uncover; her son's daughter or her daughter's daughter you shall not take to uncover her nakedness. They are her flesh; it is wickedness.
A woman to her sister you shall not take as rival, to uncover her nakedness beside her while she lives.
To a woman in impurity of her menstruation/separation you shall not draw near to uncover her nakedness.
To wife of your companion you shall not give your emission for seed, becoming unclean with her.
From your seed you shall not give to cause to pass over to Molech; you shall not profane name of your Divine. I am YHWH.
With a male you shall not lie lyings of a woman; it is taboo/ritually abhorrent.
With any beast you shall not give your emission, becoming unclean with it; and a woman shall not stand before beast to lie with it; it is confusion/violation.
Do not make yourselves unclean by any of these, for by all these nations I am sending from before you became unclean.
The land became unclean, and I visited its iniquity upon it, and land vomited out its inhabitants.
But you shall keep my statutes and my judgments, and not do any of these taboos/ritually abhorrent things, native or sojourner who sojourns in your midst,
for all these taboos/ritually abhorrent things people of land did who were before you, and land became unclean.
Let not land vomit you out when you make it unclean, as it vomited nation before you.
For everyone who does any of these taboos/ritually abhorrent things, the souls/lives doing them shall be cut off from midst of their people.
You shall keep my charge, not doing any of taboo statutes done before you, and not make yourselves unclean by them. I am YHWH your Divine.

translation_notes: This chapter is a sexual-boundary holiness text with kinship, exploitation, rival-wife, menstruation, adultery, child-sacrifice, male-male, and animal-boundary material; it requires careful historical and ethical audit.
'Uncover nakedness' is idiomatic sexual-boundary language often tied to kinship honor and household integrity.
Molech/child-sacrifice polemic is life-protection and should not be blurred with other categories.

Leviticus 18:22 is rendered close to source wording and flagged for deep lexical, context, and reception-history audit. It must not be used to justify hatred, violence, bullying, or dehumanization of LGBTQ people.
Land-vomit rhetoric is covenant-land holiness language and must not be used for modern ethnic cleansing or religious violence.
safety_note: Do not weaponize Leviticus 18 against LGBTQ people, women, bodies, menstruation, survivors, or outsiders. The restored layer preserves a difficult ancient holiness-boundary text; interpretation must resist hatred, coercion, sexual violence, and modern identity-based targeting.
